summary: Children of overindulgent mothers may show signs of misbehavior including aggression and frequent temper outbursts because they often receive excessive leniency which fails to establish necessary limits or consequences for their actions. This environment typically results in less disciplined conduct compared to those who experience more structured upbringing settings. \boxed{} Agent2 reasoned that children brought up by overly permissive parents are prone to display aggressive tendencies and emotional instability owing to insufficient disciplinary measures during formative years; these characteristics were then condensed into two concise statements highlighting the connection between parenting style and resultant child behavior patterns.
